name: MYTHOS GOD MODE — 16-Phase Autonomous Security Orchestrator
description: The flagship autonomous pipeline (Intel Sweep, Cyber Brain, Tool Generation, ASPM, Threat Hunt, Zero Trust Sweep, Compliance Refresh, CISO Intel Pack, SOAR Deployment, Metrics Hydration, Revenue Triggers, Adversarial Red Team, Predictive Forecast, Self-Healing Response) exposed via a dedicated dashboard, a homepage widget, and cron.

frontend.evidence: frontend/god-mode.html:264 (const GM = '/api/mythos/god-mode', calls /status,/ciso,/aspm,/compliance,/hunt-pack,/run). frontend/intel.html:993-1491 embeds a second, independent God Mode panel (triggerGodMode() line 1461, loadGodModeStatus() line 1368). frontend/index.html has a homepage status widget (~2124-2156, initGodModeWidget ~11703) and an Agentic AI Console tab (_loadMythos ~16329, triggerMythos ~16479). frontend/ciso-hub.html:365 links to frontend/god-mode.html; frontend/soc-operations.html:146,283 and frontend/soc-agents.html:124 link to /god-mode (anchor text relabeled 'APEX AI Copilot').

navigation.evidence: docs/capability-registry/domains/navigation.json's existing CAP-NAV-001 entry independently states GOD MODE is a top-level nav link visible to every anonymous visitor with no plan-based visibility logic (frontend/index.html:1184 desktop pill, :1234 mobile drawer, :2155 homepage CTA, :7740 directory card) — gated at the destination page/API instead of at the nav layer.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/adminKeyConsolidation.test.mjs:10 imports handleGodModeRun and asserts the 403/allow gate. workers/test/godModeIntelContract.test.mjs:15-16 cross-checks workers/src/services/mythosGodMode.js's Phase-1 SQL against workers/schema_v40_godmode_intel_columns.sql. workers/test/godModeDashboard.test.mjs static-parses frontend/god-mode.html for all 7 endpoints + admin-key gating. workers/test/mythosWidgetHonesty.test.mjs static-parses frontend/index.html's _loadMythos/triggerMythos for no-fabricated-numbers + success-checking. FIXED 2026-07-12: workers/test/godModeRbacWiring.test.mjs (8 tests) — confirms the two pre-existing paths (ADMIN_KEY, ENTERPRISE tier) are unchanged, confirms the new admin:infra:operate path admits a SUPERADMIN-role or owner-email authCtx, confirms a non-SUPERADMIN role list is still denied, and confirms /ciso and /hunt-pack now work correctly called with no authCtx argument at all.

verification.evidence: First audit of this domain (previously an unpopulated [] stub). workers/src/services/mythosGodMode.js and workers/src/handlers/mythosGodModeHandler.js read directly (1409+173 lines); workers/src/index.js:5180-5209 read directly for all 7 routes; workers/src/auth/rbac.js:103 read directly for the 'admin:infra:operate' permission; frontend/god-mode.html read directly including its own code comments at lines 137-142 and 797-824 documenting a prior RBAC-0 (no gate at all) finding that has since been fixed with a real tier-or-staff gate via frontend/assets/staff-auth.js's CDB_STAFF_AUTH.guard(); workers/test/*.mjs matches confirmed by direct grep+read. FIXED 2026-07-12: workers/src/handlers/mythosGodModeHandler.js edited directly — handleGodModeRun now also accepts the admin:infra:operate permission (via workers/src/auth/rbac.js's can()) as a third, additive path alongside the two pre-existing ones (ADMIN_KEY, ENTERPRISE tier); neither pre-existing path was narrowed. workers/src/index.js:5203-5212 edited directly to remove the dead resolveAuthV5 calls on the /ciso and /hunt-pack routes, matching the already-unauthenticated /status,/compliance,/aspm routes. Full suite green: 255 files / 2615 tests. Registry validator: 0 failures, 0 warnings.

notes: Only the /run trigger is actually access-gated (isValidAdminKey() OR tier===ENTERPRISE OR admin:infra:operate, plus a 409 concurrent-run guard) — the 6 read routes (status/report/ciso/hunt-pack/compliance/aspm) are public by deliberate design per frontend/api-docs.html:730 ('All read endpoints are public; the run trigger requires an x-api-key admin key'), including the CISO Intel Pack and Board Report content. FIXED 2026-07-12: the two open findings from the prior pass are both closed. (1) The dead auth plumbing on /ciso and /hunt-pack (resolveAuthV5 called but never read by the handler bodies) is removed — both routes now call their handlers with no authCtx argument, matching /status,/compliance,/aspm. (2) workers/src/auth/rbac.js:103's 'admin:infra:operate' permission (declared with the inline comment '// god-mode / autonomous orchestration' but never imported or referenced anywhere, grep-confirmed before this fix) is now wired into handleGodModeRun as a third valid path via that file's can() export, additive only — a real named SUPERADMIN-role user or the legacy owner-email path can now trigger a run without needing the shared ADMIN_KEY secret or an ENTERPRISE subscription; neither of the two pre-existing paths was narrowed or removed. `rbac.enforced` moves to `true` and `rbac.permissions` now lists `admin:infra:operate` accordingly. Priority stays P4 per this file's historical-severity convention — the gap this priority reflected is now closed, but the number itself is not renumbered down after the fact. schema_v33_mythos_god_mode.sql's mythos_god_mode_runs table is confirmed dead — never referenced anywhere under workers/src (grep-confirmed); the real pipeline reads/writes the plainer, earlier mythos_runs table (schema_v32) instead, shared with CAP-MYTHOS-002. Two naming collisions to never conflate with this capability: (1) 'GOD MODE vNN' is also a generic internal release-wave section-header codename used ~14 times in workers/src/index.js for unrelated features (Delivery Engine, MCP Control, Revenue Autopilot, etc.) — none import any mythos*.js file; (2) 'MYTHOS' is also the customer-facing AI-chat-widget brand name (frontend/index.html's 'MYTHOS AI Analyst' → POST /api/ai/chat → handleAIChat in aiAnalysis.js; user-dashboard's 'You are MYTHOS' system prompt → POST /api/ai/analyze) — neither handler lives in or imports a mythos*.js file, it is UI branding only. 2026-07-11 (Tier-2 backlog item #3): a THIRD naming collision found and fixed in that same 'MYTHOS AI Analyst' UI element — two badges (frontend/index.html's chat-panel header and its 'ONLINE · ...' status line) additionally claimed the panel was 'POWERED BY CYBERBRAIN V2' / 'CyberBrain V2 active'. This is a fourth, unrelated engine (workers/src/core/cyberBrain.js / services/cyberBrainEngine.js, real, versioned v20.0 in its own source, used for scan-result enrichment — see enrichScanWithBrain) that the chat panel's real backend (handleAIChat, a template-based intent/response builder) never imports or calls; 'V2' matched no real version of anything. A third occurrence of the identical 'CYBERBRAIN V2' string on the separate Autonomous SOC Mode badge (same page) was fixed identically. All three now read plain 'MYTHOS' branding, matching what the backend actually is.

name: MYTHOS Orchestrator Core (legacy v1.0 defense-tool generation pipeline)
description: The original tool-generation pipeline (Sigma/YARA/KQL/Suricata/IR-playbook/STIX generation with self-correction retries) that God Mode's Phase 3 now delegates to; still independently reachable via its own legacy routes and homepage widget.

frontend.evidence: initMythosUI() (~line 15479) fetches GET /api/mythos/status; 'Generate Defense Pack' button p13TriggerMythos() (~15518-15528) POSTs /api/mythos/run — the older, pre-God-Mode homepage integration point, still present alongside the newer God Mode widget.

navigation.evidence: Reachable as an embedded homepage widget, not a nav link of its own — same page as CAP-MYTHOS-001's homepage widget.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/adminKeyConsolidation.test.mjs:12,72-75 imports handleMythosRun directly and asserts authCtx.role==='admin' bypasses without the x-admin-key header.

verification.evidence: workers/src/services/mythosOrchestrator.js and workers/src/handlers/mythosHandler.js read directly; workers/src/index.js:5128-5154 read directly for all 6 routes including the inline x-admin-key-only pre-check at 5129-5131; frontend/index.html's initMythosUI/p13TriggerMythos read directly; workers/test/adminKeyConsolidation.test.mjs read directly.

notes: Real auth inconsistency, not a false-negative: workers/src/index.js runs its own inline gate on POST /api/mythos/run using only request.headers.get('x-admin-key') (line 5129-5131) *before* ever calling handleMythosRun, then passes a hardcoded {role:'admin'} authCtx. This is narrower than the centralized isValidAdminKey() helper that handleMythosRun itself imports and would otherwise use (auth/middleware.js:306-314 also accepts x-api-key/x-admin-secret/Authorization:Bearer) — that broader helper is effectively unreachable on this specific route because the stricter outer check runs first. Low customer impact (this is the legacy path superseded by CAP-MYTHOS-001) but worth a one-line fix (delete the redundant inline check, let handleMythosRun's own isValidAdminKey() gate it) when this domain reaches an active fix wave. Shares the mythos_runs D1 table with CAP-MYTHOS-001 (schema_v32); runMythosCron fires every 12h as an explicit 'legacy fallback' alongside God Mode's 6h cron (workers/src/index.js:9305-9313).

name: MYTHOS Revenue Engine (real domain-scan + compliance-benchmark, paywall-aware)
description: Paywall-aware domain-scan and compliance-gap endpoints. As of 2026-07-12 both are thin wrappers around this platform's real engines (live DNS/TLS/DNSBL scan; framework-benchmark compliance) instead of fabricated data. The multi-rail checkout previously in this file has been removed — see notes.

frontend.evidence: No frontend file calls /api/mythos/scan or /api/mythos/compliance (grepped across all frontend/*.html). A separate, unreachable dead-code UI (frontend/index.html's #mythosOverlay modal + window.CDB_MYTHOS, ~lines 23082-23353) called the now-removed /api/mythos/checkout/initialize; confirmed zero callers of CDB_MYTHOS.open() anywhere (grepped all onclick/JS call sites), so removing that backend route broke nothing live. Flagged as a follow-up cleanup, not fixed in this pass (out of scope — the checkout removal, not frontend deletion, was the approved change).

navigation.evidence: No nav entry or page reaches this backend capability.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/mythosRevenueEngineRealEngines.test.mjs (13 tests): proves handleMythosScan calls the real resolveDomain/inferTLSGrade/fullBlacklistCheck chain with the submitted target, that two different targets produce genuinely different results, that none of the old fabricated IOC/rule-template strings are emitted, that a live-DNS failure falls back to the honest deterministic engine (not a crash or a fabricated verdict), and that free/premium gating of findings + the mythos_intelligence block works. Proves handleMythosCompliance uses the real complianceEngine (honest STATIC/benchmark labeling, never the old fake 'ALIGNED' claim) and rejects unsupported frameworks. Also statically confirms handleMythosCheckout/handleMythosWebhook are gone from both the handler file and the route table.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/mythosRevenueEngine.js rewritten and read directly in full (v31.0.0, 214 lines). workers/src/lib/dns.js (resolveDomain:208, inferTLSGrade:245), workers/src/lib/dnsbl.js (fullBlacklistCheck:216), workers/src/handlers/domain.js (buildRealResult:262, confirmed real via live DoH/HEAD-probe/DNSBL fetch calls and an honest 'unmeasurable' non-verdict when DNS doesn't resolve), workers/src/engine.js (complianceEngine:652, domainScanEngine:49, both read in full), workers/src/services/mythosEnrichmentEngine.js (enrichAssessmentWithMYTHOS:189, real MITRE mapping/CyberBrain risk scoring/attack-path prediction/AI narrative gated on CRITICAL+HIGH findings) all read directly and reused rather than re-implemented. workers/src/index.js:5161-5171 read directly for the surviving 2 routes; the checkout/webhook route block and their import lines confirmed removed. workers/test/mythosRevenueEngineRealEngines.test.mjs written and passing (13/13); full suite 246 files/2508 tests green; registry validator 0/0.

notes: 2026-07-12 — CAP-MYTHOS-003 full remediation (item 2 of the 22-paid-feature production program, resumed after a usage-limit cutoff mid-investigation). Prior state (2026-07-11 audit) was worse than 'missing frontend': handleMythosScan pulled a generic KV intel cache (not target-specific) and returned 100%-hardcoded Sigma/KQL/Suricata/YARA/SOC-playbook string templates plus a fixed 4-IP IOC list, identical for any target once the KV cache was empty, branded 'CYBERDUDEBIVASH MYTHOS AI v30.0.2' — live and reachable with zero authentication (isPremium() silently treats a missing authCtx as free-tier rather than rejecting the call). handleMythosCompliance returned a static per-framework lookup table with verification_status hardcoded 'ALIGNED' for every organization. Fixed by redirecting both to this platform's real, already-proven engines: handleMythosScan now calls resolveDomain/inferTLSGrade/fullBlacklistCheck (live DNS-over-HTTPS + TLS header probe + 7-feed DNSBL check, the same chain serving paying customers on /api/scan/domain) and enriches via enrichAssessmentWithMYTHOS (real MITRE ATT&CK mapping, CyberBrain risk scoring, attack-path prediction, autonomous remediation plan, AI executive narrative) for premium tiers — genuinely computed value in place of the deleted static templates; a live-DNS failure falls back to domainScanEngine (an honest heuristic engine, not a fabricated verdict), never to the old fake content. handleMythosCompliance now calls complianceEngine (workers/src/engine.js), the same industry-benchmark engine already serving 6 live frontend pages via compliance.js — self-labeled assessment_mode:'STATIC'/live_verification:false rather than a false 'verified ALIGNED' claim. Separately, handleMythosCheckout/handleMythosWebhook (multi-rail UPI/Bank/Crypto/Razorpay checkout) were removed entirely, not redirected, after investigation (3 parallel research passes) found: (a) zero frontend callers of the real route — the one seeming match, frontend/index.html's #mythosOverlay modal calling /api/mythos/checkout/initialize via window.CDB_MYTHOS.open(), is itself unreachable dead code (nothing anywhere calls .open() on it; every real 'Buy'/'Subscribe' button on the platform already goes through the separate, real window.CDB_CHECKOUT_MODAL system wired to /api/payment/create-order+verify); (b) handleMythosCheckout's own Razorpay branch never minted a real Razorpay order, so it could not legitimately be used as-is; (c) its webhook wrote real tier upgrades (UPDATE users SET tier=... WHERE id=?) to the exact same column the platform's actual billing path (payments.js + subscriptionPaywallEngine.js) uses, verified by the same shared RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET, through separate, never-tested logic — a live, latent, duplicate tier-grant mechanism with no product behind it. Owner explicitly chose removal over disabling-in-place or leaving as documented risk. Two follow-ups found but deliberately left alone (out of scope for this fix): (1) frontend/index.html's dead #mythosOverlay/CDB_MYTHOS block (~lines 23082-23353) still exists and could be deleted in a future pass; (2) workers/index.ts + workers/webhook.ts + workers/health.js + workers/trust-center.js + workers/cve-ingester.js are a second, fully orphaned set of files (own SENTINEL_DB/SENTINEL_CACHE bindings, a genuinely well-built HMAC+replay-protected Razorpay webhook) never bundled by the actual deploy — workers/wrangler.toml's main="src/index.js" confirms only workers/src/index.js is ever deployed. Not touched here since they're inert (not part of the build), but worth an explicit decision (delete vs. document vs. actually wire up) in a future session. auth_enforced/rbac.enforced corrected to false (previously mis-recorded true): neither route ever rejects an unauthenticated caller — isPremium(authCtx) only gates response depth, an intentional design choice already used elsewhere on this platform, unchanged by this fix.

name: MYTHOS Platform Governor (autonomous health monitor + auto-repair)
description: Runs 9 parallel subsystem health checks (database, KV, threat intel, God Mode staleness, auth, revenue, AI provider, scan engines, Phase B products) every 6 hours, auto-repairs threat-intel staleness and God-Mode stalls, and sends Telegram alerts.

frontend.evidence: grep -i "governor" across all of frontend/ returns 0 files, 0 matches. Purely backend/cron-driven with a real, admin-gated API but no UI consumer of any kind.

navigation.evidence: Not applicable — no page exists for this capability to be linked from.

test_coverage.evidence: No workers/test/*.mjs file imports from workers/src/services/mythosGovernor.js.

verification.evidence: workers/src/services/mythosGovernor.js read directly (609 lines: runPlatformGovernor:410, handleGovernorStatus:535, handleGovernorReport:587). workers/src/index.js:5288-5300 read directly — /status and /report both require authCtx.isAdmin || tier==='ENTERPRISE' (handler-level, lines 536-537/588-589); /run has a stricter inline admin-only check in workers/src/index.js itself with no ENTERPRISE bypass (line 5299), the strongest auth story of any capability in this domain. workers/schema_v38_governor.sql read directly, confirmed governor_events is the real table runPlatformGovernor writes to (workers/src/services/mythosGovernor.js:59-66,503-513).

notes: This is an internal ops/reliability tool by construction, not a customer-facing gap — 'no frontend' here is correctly scored as a test-coverage gap (P6), not a P2 frontend-missing customer-facing gap, since nothing about this capability is meant to be customer-visible. docs/audit-history/MYTHOS_PLATFORM_GOVERNOR.md is a genuinely accurate, detailed design doc matching the code closely, including a 2026-06-11 live-validation note — one of the few audit-history docs in this repo confirmed still trustworthy on direct comparison to code.

name: MYTHOS AI Provider / "APEX NEXUS" (multi-provider AI routing library)
description: A shared library (executive narrative, threat attribution, remediation narrative, code-security analysis, forensics investigation, CVE research, red-team simulation, compliance narrative generation) that routes through a Groq to DeepSeek to Together AI to CF Workers AI to OpenRouter to Anthropic provider chain with an 8s deadline and prompt-injection defenses.

frontend.evidence: This is a shared internal library — its 8 generator functions are called server-side by other handlers (red team, forensics, code review, compliance narrative generation), not exposed as routes themselves; no direct frontend caller was found for the 2 health-check routes either.

navigation.evidence: Not applicable — internal library, not a page.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/mythosAIProviderDeadline.test.mjs imports all 8 generator functions directly and asserts each bounds routeAICall to <=8000ms (regression test for a real production incident: the 12000ms router default previously exceeded the frontend's 8s hard timeout). workers/test/mythosAIProviderInjectionDefense.test.mjs imports the same 8 functions and asserts each uses frameUntrustedInput()/UNTRUSTED_INPUT_POLICY and redacts secrets from AI output (OWASP LLM01 defense).

verification.evidence: workers/src/core/mythosAIProvider.js read directly (404 lines, all 9 named exports with line numbers). workers/src/index.js:5868-5879 read directly for the 2 public routes. workers/test/mythosAIProviderDeadline.test.mjs and workers/test/mythosAIProviderInjectionDefense.test.mjs both read directly, confirmed real imports of the actual functions (not mocks).

notes: The best-tested capability in this entire domain — two real regression-guard test suites tied to actual past production incidents (timeout overrun, prompt-injection defense). No auth/RBAC/subscription gating is appropriate here since this is plumbing consumed by other, separately-gated handlers, not a customer-facing entry point of its own. Priority set to P7 (documentation) only because this library's role and consumers aren't described in any canonical doc outside this registry entry — not because of any functional gap.

name: MYTHOS AI Enrichment Engine (cross-cutting scan-report enrichment)
description: Adds MITRE ATT&CK mapping, Cyber Brain risk/attack-path/threat-actor analysis, an AI executive narrative, an autonomous remediation plan, and a 'MYTHOS Authority Seal' to any engine's raw report; persists the enriched report to service_assessments.

frontend.evidence: Not a route of its own — the most widely-embedded piece of the subsystem, called directly from workers/src/services/consultationPreAssessEngine.js:14,295; workers/src/services/serviceOrderEngine.js:23,177; workers/src/handlers/compliance.js:17,49; workers/src/handlers/ai.js:5,43; workers/src/handlers/aiSPMHandlers.js:15,248; workers/src/handlers/domain.js:19,436; workers/src/handlers/serviceHandlers.js:33 plus 12 call sites (176,194,230,253,292,346,401,426,452,473,494,515); workers/src/handlers/identity.js:17,49; workers/src/handlers/redteam.js:5,34. FIXED 2026-07-13 for the highest-traffic path: frontend/index.html's renderResults() (~line 9866), the function that renders every homepage domain/ai/redteam/identity/compliance scan result, previously discarded the mythos_intelligence field entirely — real, already-computed MITRE mapping, CyberBrain risk signals/attack-path prediction, AI executive narrative and autonomous remediation plan were returned by the API and never rendered. New _buildMythosIntelligenceSection() (~line 10089) now renders it: full detail when data.is_premium_locked===false, a locked preview pointing to the same report-unlock CTA otherwise. Still open: the other consumers (workers/src/services/consultationPreAssessEngine.js, workers/src/services/serviceOrderEngine.js, workers/src/handlers/aiSPMHandlers.js, workers/src/handlers/serviceHandlers.js — consultation/PDF-report/order flows, not the homepage widget) were not re-traced in this pass and may still not surface the field to their own UIs.

navigation.evidence: Indirect only — reachability depends entirely on the 9 consuming handlers' own navigation, which was not individually re-traced in this pass. The homepage renderResults() path (see frontend evidence above) is confirmed discoverable — it's the primary scan-result view every visitor sees.

test_coverage.evidence: No test file directly imports workers/src/services/mythosEnrichmentEngine.js itself; its downstream effects are indirectly exercised by whichever tests exist for the 9 consuming handler files, not independently verified in this pass. FIXED 2026-07-13: workers/test/mythosIntelligenceUIRendering.test.mjs (6 tests) statically locks the new frontend/index.html rendering — confirms renderResults() calls _buildMythosIntelligenceSection(), confirms the locked/FREE-tier branch returns before any premium block (AI narrative, attack paths, MITRE mapping, remediation plan) renders, and confirms AI-generated narrative text and MITRE/threat-actor fields are HTML-escaped before interpolation (XSS defense-in-depth against LLM output).

verification.evidence: workers/src/services/mythosEnrichmentEngine.js read directly (339 lines: enrichAssessmentWithMYTHOS:189, enrichAndPersistAssessment:302). workers/schema_v37_mythos_integration.sql read directly, confirmed mythos_enriched/mythos_confidence columns added to service_assessments (lines 7-8) and 8 named services upgraded to automated/hybrid delivery. All 9 consuming call sites confirmed by direct grep across the listed files. 2026-07-13: workers/src/handlers/domain.js:434-446 read directly, confirming enrichAssessmentWithMYTHOS runs unconditionally on every /api/scan/domain call (the real, cached, history-tracked homepage scan path — not the separate, weaker CAP-MYTHOS-003 /api/mythos/scan route). workers/src/middleware/monetization.js's addMonetizationFlags read directly, confirming it spreads mythos_intelligence through untouched (only findings/locked_findings are tier-gated at the API layer) — so the new frontend gate in _buildMythosIntelligenceSection (keyed off the same data.is_premium_locked flag the rest of the page already uses) is the only tier gate on the rendered UI, matching the page's existing findings-lock convention rather than inventing a new one. frontend/index.html's renderResults() and the new _buildMythosIntelligenceSection()/_mythosEsc() read directly in full. Full suite green: 271 files / 2796 tests (the 2 Playwright E2E specs that vitest also attempted to collect in this pass fail to import — expected, they require the separate @playwright/test install used only by the dedicated E2E CI job, not workers/vitest.config.mjs). Registry validator: 0 hard failures, 0 warnings, 97 capability ids across 21 domain files.

notes: Correctly has no dedicated UI of its own — it is plumbing that enriches other capabilities' reports, not a standalone feature page. auth_enforced/rbac/navigation marked "unknown" rather than false because gating is inherited entirely from whichever of the 9 consuming handlers is invoked, and this pass did not re-verify each of those 9 handlers' own auth individually. 2026-07-13 (EPIS #6, Global Product Dominance Program session): investigated CAP-MYTHOS-003's flagged 'missing frontend' gap for /api/mythos/scan and /api/mythos/compliance first; found those two routes are a redundant, strictly weaker subset of the already-live, already-frontend-wired /api/scan/domain and compliance.js paths (no response caching, no scan-history tracking, no adaptive-brain enrichment) — building a dedicated page for them would have added a sixth 'duplicate system' smell to a codebase that already documents five duplicate API-key implementations as a known issue. Redirected the effort to this capability instead: the SAME real mythos_intelligence payload is already computed on every homepage scan (domain/ai/redteam/identity/compliance all call enrichAssessmentWithMYTHOS per the call-site list above) and was being silently dropped at render time on the platform's highest-traffic surface. Fixing the render path turns already-paid-for AI compute into visible customer differentiation with zero backend changes, zero new routes, and zero payment/checkout code touched. Previously the one concrete, actionable gap was no direct test coverage — now partially closed by mythosIntelligenceUIRendering.test.mjs for the new UI path specifically; a focused unit suite on enrichAssessmentWithMYTHOS's own output shape (covering all 9 consumers at once) remains a separate, still-open follow-up.
